_comment: Project-scoped settings shipped with the evolve-loop plugin. Five PreToolUse hooks enforce structural trust boundaries: (1) ship-gate on Bash — only legacy/scripts/lifecycle/ship.sh or the native 'evolve ship' CLI can perform ship-class git/gh ops (v8.13.0). (2) phase-gate-pre on Bash — subagent-run.sh invocations must follow Scout→Builder→Auditor sequence (v8.13.1). (3) role-gate on Edit|Write — files can only be modified by the active phase's agent within its allowlisted paths (v8.13.1). (4) research-quota-gate on WebSearch|WebFetch|Bash — counts research-tool calls per agent (v10.16.0). (5) doc-deletion-guard on Bash — blocks rm/mv on docs/** or knowledge-base/** without canonical archival mv (v10.17.0). v11.4.0 routes all five through legacy/scripts/guards/evolve-guard-dispatch.sh which prefers the native Go binary ('evolve guard <name>') and falls back to the bash legacy scripts when the binary is missing or EVOLVE_NATIVE_GUARDS=0. All gates honor an emergency bypass env var; routine bypassing is a CLAUDE.md violation. PostToolUse on Edit|Write syntax-checks the modified file by extension; never blocks (PostToolUse can't), only emits WARN to LLM stderr (v8.13.3).
